era: 1989-1996 core. Grunge/Psych. E=6 -- grunge frame over psychedelic substrate. U-District. Sub Pop. Fourth appearance.
associated_musicians: Presidents of the United States of America (Jason Finn)

theory_nodes.concept: Fourth appearance arc: 221 (Psychedelic, E=5), 299 (return, E=5), 306 (Grunge/Psych, E=6). 85-degree span from first to current. Energy escalates. Genre expands to compound classification.
theory_nodes.description: The engine's most-returned band in the current cluster.

theory_nodes.concept: Grunge/Psych compound genre. Dayglo (1992): Out of Focus = heavy riff + dreamy middle + heavy return. Both grunge structure and psychedelic texture simultaneously. The overlap is real, not diplomatic.
theory_nodes.description: First compound genre assignment for Love Battery.

theory_nodes.concept: Ron Nine = third Seattle guitar vocabulary: psychedelic expansiveness. Thayil = metal precision. Peterson = punk immediacy. Nine = fuzz-as-texture, reverb-as-depth. The guitar as environment.
theory_nodes.description: Three distinct Seattle lead guitar approaches documented.

theory_nodes.concept: U-District inside the grunge cluster. Capitol Hill majority (294-308) plus Love Battery as U-District representative. Sub Pop geographic spread across Seattle neighborhoods.
theory_nodes.description: U-District enters the grunge cluster through Love Battery.

cross_references.relationship: First appearance E=5. Psychedelic Rock. First U-District band. Sub Pop psychedelic wing. FIRST ACCIPITRIDAE pair (raptor era begins).
Third appearance E=5. Late Sub Pop era. 78-degree gap from 221.
Consecutive grunge-cluster entry. Mark Lanegan. Ellensburg to Seattle to Pacific Northwest.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=6 + E=8 = E=14. Maximum asymmetry in current zone. The sea lion battle is the more intense event; the music is the psychedelic-heavy substrate.
